title: calophyllum ferrugineum var. oblongifolium
intro: Calophyllum ferrugineum var. oblongifolium, also known as the Red Balau, is a variant of the Calophyllum ferrugineum tree, native to the tropical regions of Southeast Asia. This plant has been traditionally used in folk medicine due to its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties. The health benefits of Calophyllum ferrugineum var. oblongifolium include its potential to treat various ailments such as fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, as well as its ability to boost the immune system and exhibit anti-cancer properties. The herbal preparations of this plant often involve infusing its leaves or bark in hot water to create a tea, or mixing its extracts with other herbs to create a tonic or ointment.
